_comment: Routes by task class (per the spec's 'Routing'). The COO classifies inline (class) → this config deterministically sets the mechanism and roles. Principle 1: builder(claude) ≠ Adversary/critics(codex). Principle 6: don't inflate a simple task.
_mechanism_note: VERIFIABLE classes (simple/first_class/complex_irreversible) → NATIVE executionPolicy: builder = executor work-issue, reviewStages = sequential review stages (the runtime drives the tail + the remediation loop: a reviewer status≠done = a return to the builder). preStages (if any) go BEFORE the builder as separate subtasks. PRODUCT_CONVERGENCE → parallel ideation (native sibling subtasks without blocks) + a merge, since there's no objective oracle.

simple.desc: 1 executor + an optional objective check (Principle 6 — don't inflate).

first_class.desc: search+verify — where the system is strongest.

reviewStages.note: codex ≠ builder(claude) — decorrelation (Principle 1); a critic with the right to return for rework

complex_irreversible.desc: Rubric Audit BEFORE generation + the full review tail + a MANDATORY handoff.

preStages.note: BEFORE the builder: an audit of criteria — protection against optimizing the wrong target

product_convergence.desc: there's NO objective oracle → parallel ideation from different angles + disagreement-as-signal + the Operator's taste.

_intake_note: The COO classifies inline first; then verifiable classes pass SpecWriter → SpecReviewer (BEFORE the route). For a simple task the spec-gate may be skipped (Principle 6, classify-first).
